title: Differential Viability
question: When everyone can do this, what is the value of me doing it?
whatsUnderThreat: The portion of current compensation and demand that comes from rarity rather than from durable skill. The skill premium for early adoption is by definition temporary.

reading.text: Skill-rarity premium contracting faster than projected. Inbound mix has shifted toward capability-acquisition asks (47% in May, up from 29% in January). Pricing power holding indexed, but composition tells the cleaner story. Published with the same visibility a favorable trend would receive.

measurements.question: How is my engagement pricing changing over time, normalized to comparable scope?
measurements.method: Invoice and contract records, normalized by an explicit scope index.

measurements.question: What kind of questions are prospects bringing me — capability-acquisition asks, or next-order questions?
measurements.method: AI classifier on inbound email subject lines, first-call notes, calendar-invite descriptions. Categories: capability acquisition / next-order strategic / referral introduction / other.

measurements.question: How widely is my practice's distinctive vocabulary diffusing through public discourse?
measurements.method: Track curated list of phrases championed by the practice across X, LinkedIn, Mastodon, blog corpora, podcast transcripts.

measurements.question: What can I do today that I couldn't two years ago — and what fraction is still rare?
measurements.method: Annual reflection paired with an external scan of how widely the named capabilities have diffused into the field.
